element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to select destinations and manage two-pilot team on Lylat system map
Player discovers they can swap between two chosen pilots with independent shield gauges
Arwing combat against basic enemy fighters on action screen
Player must intercept interplanetary ballistic missiles before they reach Corneria
Player learns that failing to intercept threats raises Corneria's damage meter toward 100% loss condition
Battleships deploy fighter squadrons; defeating them reduces enemy production
Bases fire IPBMs; player must destroy them to reduce missile threat
General Pepper's satellite provides limited defensive fire; special enemies can hijack it
Mercenary team serves as narrative/skill-check encounters alongside main objectives
Giant bosses present major challenges; progression toward Emperor Andross
Final objective after clearing Lylat system of Andross's fleet

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to map screen after action sequence
Completing all objectives in a region
Destroying battleship before it deploys more fighters
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple simultaneous threats (fighters + missiles + advancing enemies)
Corneria damage meter rising toward critical
Enemy forces advancing while player is engaged in combat elsewhere
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Semi-real-time strategy cycle: player pauses on map to plan, then semi-real-time unfolds with simultaneous enemy movement. Action sequences resolve faster than map-screen threats advance, forcing difficult triage decisions about which threats to intercept first.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Player learns to manage two-pilot team and basic fighter interception while Corneria damage accumulates slowly
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of battleships and planetary bases multiplies threat complexity; satellite hijacking adds objectives; player must balance offensive and defensive priorities
difficulty_curve.late_game: Increased enemy density, Star Wolf encounters, and large bosses require mastery of team swapping and threat prioritization
notable_spikes: First battleship encounter (multiple simultaneous threat types)
Satellite hijacking events (forcing choice between clearing region or defending satellite)
Star Wolf team battles (skilled AI opponents)

notable_level_patterns.description: Player must balance pilot resources (two finite pilots), Corneria's health meter (one finite resource), and satellite defense. Each choice to engage one threat leaves others undefended.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any map region with simultaneous battleship + IPBM + satellite threat

notable_level_patterns.description: Paused strategic planning on map screen transitions to real-time action when ships engage enemies. Time moves at different rates across contexts, creating pressure.
Destroying battleships stops fighter generation; destroying bases stops missile launches. Player learns to target production sources rather than individual units.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-game regions with active battleships and bases
